---
title: "Infected Tomato Leaf - Vein Segmentation"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2024-09-12
date-format: long
image: "../../images/InfectedTomato.png"

categories:
  - Projects
  - ML Marathon
  - MLM24
  - Image segmentation
  - Computer vision
  - Deep learning
  - U-Net
  - Agriculture
  - Image data
  - UW-Madison

---

## About this project
The Infected Tomato Leaf - Vein Segmentation challenge was featured in the [2024 Machine Learning Marathon (MLM24)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/). This UW-Madison-originated Kaggle competition focuses on segmenting primary and secondary vein structures from tomato leaf images at varying stages of disease progression.

### Challenge design
- **Task**: Segment leaf vein structures in images of infected tomato leaves, distinguishing between primary and secondary veins across different disease stages.
- **Domain**: Agriculture and plant pathology -- automated vein segmentation can help researchers study how diseases affect leaf vascular structure.
- **Methods**: Image segmentation approaches, commonly using architectures like U-Net.

### Links
- **Kaggle challenge**: [UW-Madison Infected Tomato Leaf - Vein Segmentation](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/infected-tomato-leaf-vein-segmentation)

## See also
- [**Data**: LVD2021 Leaf Vein Dataset](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Data/LVD2021_Leaf-Vein-Dataset.html): A related dataset for leaf vein analysis that can complement this challenge.
- [**Model**: U-Net](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/UNET.html): Learn about U-Net, a popular architecture for biomedical and agricultural image segmentation tasks.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this project, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.
